project.title: El papa que juzgó un cadáver

visual.image_prompt: CLEAR LITERAL SCENE — it must SHOW Pope Formosus as a tasteful non-graphic corpse seated upright on a papal throne while Pope Stephen VI points at him and bishops recoil with huge shocked eyes; a torchlit medieval Roman church hall with stone arches, candles, legal scrolls and deep shadows in foreground, midground and background; shocked, impossible-trial mood. Rich painterly 2D cartoon illustration with soft shading and textured brushwork, hand-painted look, bold clean outlines, soft natural light. NOT 3D, NOT Pixar, NOT dark, NOT cluttered. Funny minimalist white stick-figure people: round off-white heads, thin black stick limbs, simple oval bodies, big round expressive eyes, exaggerated comic faces. NOT animals. HERO RULE: the white stick-figure characters are LARGE in the foreground and the clear main subject, big and prominent with expressive faces; the setting is fully detailed BEHIND and around them with depth and props, but stays smaller than the characters and never becomes the main subject. RICH ENVIRONMENT: fill the frame with a real, specific, detailed setting with depth (clear foreground, midground and background), props and atmospheric light — NEVER a flat empty color wash behind the characters. CHARACTERIZE BY CULTURE: every human stick-figure is dressed for its role so the viewer instantly reads who they are, while KEEPING the round white stick-figure head, big eyes, NO hair, NO beard. Pope Formosus as corpse: papal white vestments with muted red and gold trim, small papal tiara, pale still round head with simple X-marks for eyes, discreetly covered and NON-GRAPHIC. Pope Stephen VI: white papal robe with muted red cloak, small papal tiara, stern angry expression. The deacon: simple dark deacon robe with white collar and a small scroll. Bishops: cream and gold medieval clerical robes, simple miters, small croziers. Roman guards: simple dark medieval tunics and short cloaks, no weapons emphasized. Roman crowd: plain medieval tunics and hoods. The narrator / a generic onlooker stays a plain white stick-figure with NO costume. Distinguish roles by costume. EVERY character is HIGHLY EXPRESSIVE and DYNAMIC: an exaggerated emotion and an active pose, never stiff; often show several stick-figures and elements interacting, not one lone figure; play with color by the moment but keep it tasteful, NOT oversaturated; make each scene feel different from the last. Tasteful and NON-GRAPHIC: convey violence or death ONLY through stylized suggestion — silhouettes, simplified stick-figures with simple X-marks for eyes, still figures, stakes, shadows, crows, smoke, dark landscape and distant shapes. NEVER explicit gore, blood, organs or realistic wounds. Suggest clearly, never show crudely. Palette: cold stone gray, candle gold and muted papal red. Camera: dramatic LOW-ANGLE CLOSE-UP. a bold hand-painted Spanish title reads "JUZGARON A UN PAPA" in clean bold flat letters with a big red arrow pointing to the central subject, NOT ornate, NOT gold-edged. SIMPLE UNCLUTTERED composition with generous negative space, readable at a glance, NOT busy. VERTICAL 9:16 short-video framing, subject centered with room for big text. Vertical 9:16, full-bleed. All text in clear bold Spanish exactly as written.

voiceover.text: [curious] Juzgaron a un papa… nueve meses después de muerto.

Rich painterly 2D cartoon illustration with soft shading and textured brushwork, hand-painted look, bold clean outlines, soft natural light. NOT 3D, NOT Pixar, NOT dark, NOT cluttered. Funny minimalist white stick-figure people: round off-white heads, thin black stick limbs, simple oval bodies, big round expressive eyes, exaggerated comic faces. NOT animals. HERO RULE: the white stick-figure characters are LARGE in the foreground and the clear main subject, big and prominent with expressive faces; the setting is fully detailed BEHIND and around them with depth and props, but stays smaller than the characters and never becomes the main subject. RICH ENVIRONMENT: fill the frame with a real, specific, detailed setting with depth (clear foreground, midground and background), props and atmospheric light — NEVER a flat empty color wash behind the characters. CHARACTERIZE BY CULTURE: every human stick-figure is dressed for its role so the viewer instantly reads who they are, while KEEPING the round white stick-figure head, big eyes, NO hair, NO beard. Pope Formosus as corpse: papal white vestments with muted red and gold trim, small papal tiara, pale still round head with simple X-marks for eyes, discreetly covered and NON-GRAPHIC. Pope Stephen VI: white papal robe with muted red cloak, small papal tiara, stern angry expression. The deacon: simple dark deacon robe with white collar and a small scroll. Bishops: cream and gold medieval clerical robes, simple miters, small croziers. Roman guards: simple dark medieval tunics and short cloaks, no weapons emphasized. Roman crowd: plain medieval tunics and hoods. The narrator / a generic onlooker stays a plain white stick-figure with NO costume. Distinguish roles by costume. EVERY character is HIGHLY EXPRESSIVE and DYNAMIC: an exaggerated emotion and an active pose, never stiff; often show several stick-figures and elements interacting, not one lone figure; play with color by the moment but keep it tasteful, NOT oversaturated; make each scene feel different from the last. Tasteful and NON-GRAPHIC: convey violence or death ONLY through stylized suggestion — silhouettes, simplified stick-figures with simple X-marks for eyes, still figures, stakes, shadows, crows, smoke, dark landscape and distant shapes. NEVER explicit gore, blood, organs or realistic wounds. Suggest clearly, never show crudely. Palette: stone gray, parchment beige and restrained gold. Camera: OVER-THE-SHOULDER WIDE SHOT. SIMPLE UNCLUTTERED composition with generous negative space, readable at a glance, NOT busy. VERTICAL 9:16 short-video framing, subject centered with room for big text. Vertical 9:16, full-bleed. Absolutely no text, no letters, no words anywhere.

tts_export.routing_note: Reel vertical menor a cinco mil caracteres: usar eleven_v3 con tags simples y pausas de texto, sin SSML.
tts_export.pronunciation_notes: Formoso: For-mo-so. Esteban sexto: no leer como Esteban VI en el TTS. Sínodo del Cadáver: marcar acento natural en SÍ-no-do.
tts_export.full_script: [curious] Juzgaron a un papa… nueve meses después de muerto. [short pause] Lo sacaron de su tumba, en plena Roma medieval. Lo vistieron con vestiduras pontificias, como si todavía gobernara. Y sentaron su cadáver en el trono. El muerto era Formoso, papa hasta el año ochocientos noventa y seis. El juez era su sucesor político, Esteban sexto. Pero el juicio no buscaba justicia: buscaba borrar autoridad. [thoughtful] Formoso había dejado actos, cargos y ordenaciones que seguían pesando. Para destruirlos, reunieron un sínodo. Cerca del cadáver, pusieron a un diácono a responder por él. Imagínate la escena: obispos escuchando cargos legales. Y en medio, un papa que ya no podía hablar. Lo acusaron de perjurio y de romper normas canónicas. El veredicto fue culpable. Le quitaron las vestiduras y anularon sus actos y ordenaciones. [appalled] Pero el intento de borrar a Formoso salió al revés. Esteban fue depuesto, encerrado, y murió estrangulado en prisión. Después, Formoso fue rehabilitado. La memoria que quisieron matar sobrevivió. [thoughtful] Fue venganza, sí… pero también miedo a un muerto.
